root_text.sn37.31:1.4: Na, bhikkhave, mātugāmo rūpabalahetu vā bhogabalahetu vā ñātibalahetu vā puttabalahetu vā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.sn37.31:1.5: Sīlabalahetu kho, bhikkhave, mātugāmo kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.sn37.31:1.6: Imāni kho, bhikkhave, pañca mātugāmassa balānī”ti.

translation_text.sn37.31:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these five powers of a lady.
translation_text.sn37.31:1.3: Attractiveness, wealth, relatives, children, and ethical behavior.
translation_text.sn37.31:1.4: It is not because of the powers of attractiveness, wealth, relatives, or children that ladies, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.sn37.31:1.5: It is because of the power of ethical behavior that ladies, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.sn37.31:1.6: These are the five powers of a lady.”
